interface: Claude Desktop with Filesystem MCP + Windows-MCP Shell + Chrome MCP
sessionQuality: MAJOR MILESTONE — Full-stack AggDB frontend+API built, deployed to IGS-Intrusion, secured with Windows Auth. Rebecca has data entry access.
summary: Built complete AggDB data entry system from scratch after evaluating Sonnet's v3 frontend (which had significant schema mismatches: hallucinated QAMSCert table, wrong column names DocType/DocDate/FilePath/ResultValue vs actual RecTypeID/RecDate/RecLink/TestValue). Created FastAPI backend aligned to real v2 schema including all link tables (RecLink, StatusLink, TestResultLink, QAMSLink). Fixed link table PK inserts (business PKs are NOT auto-increment — needed next_id() helper). Built v4 frontend with Browse/Data Entry/New Site tabs, welcome modal, help button. Deployed to IGS-Intrusion via embedded Python 3.12, IIS reverse proxy on port 80 with ARR, scheduled task for auto-start. Secured with Windows Authentication. Added IsVisible to LU_RecType, hid SWPPP. Created Quick Start Guide and Cleanup Guide docs.

keyDesignDecisions: All link table PKs use next_id() helper (MAX+1) — NOT auto-increment identity columns

sys.path.insert(0, ...) in main.py for portable execution from any working directory
Server binds 0.0.0.0:8000, served through IIS reverse proxy on port 80

technicalLearnings.lesson: Sonnet 4.6 hallucinated table names (QAMSCert) and column names (DocType, ResultValue, EnteredBy) that don't exist in the schema. Built confident-looking UI around fake schema. Always verify AI-generated code against actual INFORMATION_SCHEMA before trusting.

ITD_AggDB link tables (RecLink, StatusLink, TestResultLink, QAMSLink, MaterialTypeLink) have non-identity business PKs. The 'id' column is identity but the *LinkID column requires explicit values. Use MAX+1 pattern via next_id() helper.
technicalLearnings.context: StatusLink insert failure — Cannot insert NULL into StatusLinkID

technicalLearnings.lesson: Windows-MCP Shell terminal commands often create processes that open and close too fast to populate or return output. For long-running commands or installs, prefer writing scripts to disk and executing them, or using Invoke-Command for remote execution.
technicalLearnings.context: pip install and Python dependency verification

technicalLearnings.lesson: Python embedded/portable on Windows: robocopy of a full Python install with site-packages (634MB with torch) is unreliable — stdlib modules can be missed, .pth files break. Better approach: download official embeddable zip (~15MB), uncomment 'import site' in ._pth file, bootstrap pip via get-pip.py, install only needed deps.

IGS domain GPO firewall blocks non-standard ports between machines (8000, 1433). Port 80 works because IIS is registered. Solution: IIS reverse proxy (ARR + URL Rewrite) to forward /aggdb on port 80 to localhost:8000 where the API runs.
technicalLearnings.context: Rebecca cannot reach port 8000, Test-NetConnection confirms blocked

technicalLearnings.lesson: Python process started via Invoke-Command -ComputerName dies when the PS session ends — session isolation. Start-Process also fails for long-running services. Solution: Register-ScheduledTask with SYSTEM account and -AtStartup trigger. Survives logoff and reboot.
technicalLearnings.context: AggDB API kept dying on Intrusion

technicalLearnings.lesson: IIS authorization section at app level requires: (1) unlock via appcmd, (2) Web-Url-Auth feature installed, (3) use -section:system.webServer/security/authorization NOT just /section:authorization (which hits management/authorization). Even then, web.config-based auth can conflict with ARR proxy. Safest: configure via appcmd at apphost level.
technicalLearnings.context: 500.19 errors when trying to restrict by AD user

technicalLearnings.lesson: For IIS + FastAPI reverse proxy: Windows Auth at IIS level handles who can access. SQL Server Windows Auth handles data permissions. The API process running as SYSTEM needs its own SQL login (NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with db_datareader/datawriter). User identity doesn't pass through the proxy to SQL — it's the service account.
technicalLearnings.context: 503 on /api/health after SYSTEM had no SQL access
